description_long: This object is a roughly spherical stone with a pale, beige coloration and a matte, slightly uneven surface. The front features two large, shallow depressions that resemble eye sockets, giving it a somewhat anthropomorphic appearance. Three protrusions extend from the lower part of the stone, suggesting feet or support points, while a smaller protrusion is visible at the bottom center. The surface texture is irregular, with subtle facets and a natural, weathered look, lacking any sharp edges or artificial markings. The stone appears solid and heavy, with no visible openings or cavities. Its overall form and finish suggest it could serve as a decorative or functional barrier in an urban park or pedestrian area.
description: A pale, roughly spherical stone with shallow depressions and three lower protrusions, featuring a matte, weathered surface.
description_view_0: Front view shows two large, shallow depressions and three lower protrusions, giving the stone a face-like appearance.
description_view_1: Left side view reveals the rounded profile and one of the lower protrusions, with the depressions partially visible.
description_view_2: Top view displays the smooth, dome-like surface with no visible depressions or protrusions.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the rounded form and a single lower protrusion, with depressions partially visible.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on pavement or landscaped area
attribute_pattern: Subtle, irregular mottling and faceting with no artificial carvings or regular patterns
attribute_weathering: Matte and slightly rough, suggesting moderate natural weathering but no heavy erosion or lichen
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts gently with paved surfaces but can blend with other natural stone elements or landscaping features
